hypothesis: If generative models for covalent triazine framework design are pretrained on large molecular corpora, fine-tuned on a small CTF dataset, and optimized with reinforcement learning using expert-crafted rewards, then they will generate candidates with higher validity, novelty, internal diversity, improved Fréchet ChemNet Distance, and a greater fraction passing expert filters than models trained from scratch or missing one or more of these transfer steps because chemical priors and domain constraints are effectively transferred.
